# Trim the Memory Core healthcheck to a lean liveness probe (token cost)

## Context

The Memory Core `healthcheck` output has accreted observability that doesn't belong in a hot-path liveness/readiness probe. Surfaced during the 2026-06-08 cost-safety / MC-degradation session (@tobiu: *"analyse the tool for bloat … topology: unified, we no longer have an alternative, pointless … remove last messages"*). The cost is **token-shaped on three axes**, so trimming is a direct, swarm-wide token saver.

## The Problem (three costs)

1. **Per-agent context-tax** — the healthcheck OUTPUT SCHEMA (`ai/mcp/server/memory-core/openapi.yaml`) loads into **every** agent's context at MCP tool-enumeration (`ToolService`). Every documented field is permanent per-agent context cost across the whole swarm.
2. **Hot-path compute** — the migration census **batch-scans ~17.9k Chroma collection records on every healthcheck** (`HealthService.mjs:1171` — "Scans Chroma collection metadata in batches"), for a probe called frequently (boot, self-repair, monitoring).
3. **Per-call output** — the large JSON returned each call.

## The Architectural Reality (source-confirmed)

- **`database.topology`** (`HealthService.mjs:131-160`, `projectChromaTopology`): JSDoc `:137` — *"the federated topology has been **retired** … `mode` is statically `'unified'`, `resolvedVia` statically `'engines.chroma'`."* Static constants cosplaying as a diagnostic; `coordinates` duplicates the chroma host already in `providers`.
- **`mailboxPreview`**: a degraded-mode fallback only (`null` when healthy); redundant with `list_messages`.
- **`migration`** (`:626-679` + `:1042-1171`): operator-scraped census (`perUserId`, `migrationDebt`, `untaggedCount`, `visibility`) recomputed via batch-scan every call.
- **`features.wake.gateReason`**: a multi-sentence incident string shipped in every payload.

## The Fix

- **REMOVE** `database.topology` (static post-federation-retirement) + `mailboxPreview` (redundant / degraded-only).
- **MOVE** the `migration` census to an **on-demand** surface (a dedicated `migration-status` tool/script behind a service method) — per the established *"expensive / operator-periodic observability → on-demand, never a healthcheck field"* pattern. Healthcheck stops batch-scanning collections per call.
- **TRIM** `features.wake.gateReason` (keep `gateState` / `daemonRunning` / `secondsSinceLastPulse`; move the reason to the gate-record/log) + verbose `providers.*` sub-details (`writeCanary` / `credential` / `endpoint`) + `auth` sub-objects + empty/transient blocks (`orchestrator.tasks`, `startup`, null `dream`).
- **KEEP** the lean core: `status`, `timestamp`, `connection.connected`, `features.summarization`, `providers.*.{active,local}`, `details[]`, `version`.

## Contract Ledger

| Surface | Source of authority | Proposed | Fallback / migration | Consumer |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `healthcheck.migration.*` (operator-scraped per JSDoc `:1043`) | HealthService census | move off healthcheck | new on-demand `migration-status` tool/script | operators (periodic) |
| `healthcheck.database.topology` | `projectChromaTopology` | remove | n/a (static constant) | none real |
| `healthcheck.mailboxPreview` | degraded fallback | remove | `list_messages` | agents |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `topology` + `mailboxPreview` removed from the healthcheck output **and** the openapi schema.
- [ ] `migration` census moved to an on-demand surface; healthcheck no longer batch-scans collections per call.
- [ ] `wake.gateReason` + verbose provider/auth sub-details trimmed.
- [ ] openapi `healthcheck` response schema byte-size measured before/after (the context-tax delta is the headline metric).
- [ ] HealthService unit specs updated for the trimmed shape; the on-demand migration surface gets its own spec.

## Out of Scope / Coordinate

- **#12765** (@neo-gpt — "Expose MCP runtime source/schema drift in healthcheck") moves the **opposite direction** (it *adds* freshness/drift fields). Both touch `HealthService.mjs` + the healthcheck output, so they **must be coordinated** — net healthcheck stays lean while carrying a *compact* freshness signal; avoids a `HealthService.mjs` collision. This ticket = trim; #12765 = add-freshness.
- KB / github-workflow healthchecks not changed here (MC first; same pattern can follow).

## Decision Record impact
`none` — applies an established observability-placement principle (MCP-tool-output-schema context-tax); no ADR conflict.

## Related
- #12765 (coordinate — same tool, opposite direction), #12767 (config drift), #12740 (cost-safety epic — token/cost-reduction aligned).

- 2026-06-09T16:25:20Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `d119c31` - "fix(ai): scope MCP runtime gitHead to git-domain servers (#12776)

The shared runtime freshness helper hard-coded a per-call 'git rev-parse HEAD' for all three MCP servers. Memory Core (memory/graph) and Knowledge Base (RAG) have no git domain: in a cloud / non-GitHub-VCS deployment their rootDir is not a git checkout, so the shellout produced a permanently-null field while still spawning a subprocess and shipping dead schema bytes into every agent's context.

gitHead is now gated on rootDir: omit it and the tracker never spawns git and drops gitHead from the payload. GitHub Workflow (a git-domain tool) keeps it; Memory Core + Knowledge Base opt out, leaving a digest-only, fully portable freshness signal. Also trims gitHead from the MC/KB healthcheck output schema.

Test Evidence: npm run test-unit on RuntimeFreshnessService + MC/KB/gh-workflow HealthService specs -> 79 passed.

Refs #12768"
- 2026-06-09T16:26:14Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by PR #12778

gitHead is now gated on rootDir: omit it and the tracker never spawns git and drops gitHead from the payload. Also trims gitHead from the MC/KB healthcheck output schema.

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-13T08:42:00Z

## Doc-sweep discipline for the remaining trim slices

From @neo-opus-vega's #13071 + #13075 reviews (`[RETROSPECTIVE]`): both shipped slices removed a documented healthcheck field and **orphaned its operator docs**, each caught only at review. Adding the discipline as an AC so the remaining slice (the `features.wake.gateReason` trim) doesn't repeat it:

- [ ] **Each healthcheck-field removal sweeps the operator docs that teach that field, in the same PR.** Grep `learn/agentos/**` + the field's JSDoc for the removed field name; restate the operator guidance (config/ADR-derived, or point at the replacement surface) or remove it. Orphaned docs mis-teach KB-querying agents + operators until swept.

Already applied retroactively: #13069's docs swept in `6cae767c0` (PR #13071), #13074's in `df4b15b29` (PR #13075).


- 2026-06-13T13:45:48Z @tobiu referenced in commit `2a12f1d` - "refactor(memory-core): drop static database.topology + redundant mailboxPreview from healthcheck (#13069) (#13071)

* refactor(memory-core): drop static database.topology + redundant mailboxPreview from healthcheck (#13069)

database.topology was a static post-federation-retirement constant (mode='unified', resolvedVia='engines.chroma'), never even documented in the openapi database schema. mailboxPreview was a degraded-only fallback redundant with list_messages; its 38-line openapi schema had drifted from the runtime shape.

Both fields load into every agent's context via the tool-enumeration openapi schema, so removing them is a direct per-agent context-tax cut (net -38 openapi lines). topologyConflicts (a distinct REM-axis signal) is untouched. HealthService.spec.mjs green (59 passed).

Slice of #12768; the migration-census on-demand move + gateReason trim remain tracked there.

* docs(agentos): sweep operator docs orphaned by the healthcheck database.topology + mailboxPreview removal (#13069)

Addresses @neo-opus-vega's #13071 review (code approved; docs were the only blocker). The removed database.topology healthcheck field was taught as 'the canonical topology proof' across the deployment docs; since the topology is now statically 'unified' (resolved from the engines.chroma config coordinate), restate the proof as connectivity-verified + config-guaranteed rather than healthcheck-scraped:

- MemoryCore.md: drop the database.topology block + mailboxPreview from the response-shape example, remove the dedicated database.topology field section, trim the payload summary.
- SharedDeployment.md + DeploymentCookbook.md: restate the verify-via-healthcheck step as database.connection.connected against engines.chroma.
- Day0Tutorial.md: fix the healthcheck example + drop the obsolete 'missing topology.mode' troubleshooting row (topology cannot be 'missing' when it is config-guaranteed unified)."
- 2026-06-13T14:20:02Z @tobiu referenced in commit `3a12797` - "refactor(memory-core): relocate healthcheck migration-census to an on-demand surface (#13074) (#13075)

* refactor(memory-core): relocate healthcheck migration-census to an on-demand surface (#13074)

The healthcheck ran the migration census on every call: #checkChromaMigrationState -> #scanChromaMetadata batch-reads the full memory + summary Chroma collections (batchSize 2000, ~17.9k records), an O(records) cost on a probe fired at boot/self-repair/monitoring. The census is operator-scraped (no programmatic consumer of healthcheck.migration), so it does not belong on the hot path.

Mirrors the graphLifecycleReport / GraphService.getLifecycleCensus precedent: new HealthService.getMigrationCensus({includeChroma}) (cheap SQLite always, Chroma scan opt-in) + ai/scripts/maintenance/migrationCensusReport.mjs (ai:migration-census-report; --chroma opts into the scan). Removes the migration field + Step-1.5 batch-scan from the healthcheck; the private methods + buildChromaMigrationStats remain (reached via getMigrationCensus).

migrationCensusReport.spec.mjs green; HealthService.spec.mjs green (no payload.migration assertions existed). Compute slice of #12768.

* docs(memory-core): point migration-census docs at the on-demand surface (#13074)

Addresses @neo-opus-vega's #13075 review (code approved; docs were the only blocker). The healthcheck.migration field was relocated to the on-demand getMigrationCensus() / ai:migration-census-report surface; the docs still taught the removed field:

- MultiTenantMigrationGuide.md §5 + runbook: restate the migration-census observability + verification steps to run 'npm run ai:migration-census-report' (getMigrationCensus({includeChroma})) and read graph.total, instead of scraping healthcheck.migration.untaggedCount.
- HealthService.mjs #checkMigrationState JSDoc: point at getMigrationCensus / the script, noting the census is off the healthcheck hot path.

Note: vega's cited MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md:407 cross-ref has no migration reference (verified) — nothing to sweep there.

* docs(memory-core): repoint the last MemoryCoreMcpAuth cross-ref to the on-demand migration-census (#13074)

@neo-opus-vega's Cycle-2 #13075 catch: MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md:407's cross-ref still described the removed 'healthcheck.migration.untaggedCount' operator guidance. (My earlier 'no ref there' was a wrong-path grep — the file is at learn/agentos/tooling/, not learn/agentos/ — V-B-A miss; vega had it right.) Repointed to the on-demand migration-census (ai:migration-census-report)."
### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-13T20:51:43Z

## Ticket-intake premise-check: substantially already-resolved

Picked this up to implement; the Verify-Before-Assert intake sweep found the **primary scope already delivered** by leaves filed after this ticket (2026-06-08):

| AC | Status | Delivered by |
|---|---|---|
| AC1 — remove `database.topology` + `mailboxPreview` (output + openapi) | ✅ done | #13069 → #13071 |
| AC2 — migration census → on-demand; no per-call batch-scan | ✅ done | #13074 → #13075 |

**Verified against current `dev` (source, not just commit subjects):**
- `#performHealthCheck` payload (`ai/services/memory-core/HealthService.mjs:1380-1416`) no longer contains `database.topology`, `mailboxPreview`, or `migration`; `:1087` comment confirms *"the census was relocated off the hot path."*
- `projectChromaTopology` / `mailboxPreview` are fully gone — no dead code.
- The `HealthCheckResponse` openapi schema carries no `topology` / `mailboxPreview` / `migration` (only the legitimately-kept `topologyConflicts` freshness signal from the #12772/#12776 runtime-freshness work).

**Residual = AC3 only (`wake.gateReason` + verbose provider sub-details), premise now partly contestable:**
- `gateReason` is still shipped in `features.wake` (`buildWakeFeaturesBlock:467/477`) but is **not** enumerated in the openapi schema → tiny per-call output cost only, no context-tax. Marginal.
- The verbose provider sub-details ARE still in `HealthCheckResponse` (`providers.embedding.writeCanary.*` etc. — a real per-agent context-tax). **But** `writeCanary` was added since this ticket as a genuine add_memory embedding-path degradation diagnostic (cost-safety). So "trim it" is no longer clean bloat-removal — it's a keep-vs-schema-bytes judgment that should be decided deliberately, not mechanically.

**Verdict:** `already-resolved` for the headline token wins; the AC3 residual is marginal and its trim premise needs re-evaluation (does `writeCanary`'s degradation-detection value justify its schema bytes?). I am **not** re-implementing done work nor trimming a live diagnostic on a stale premise.

**Recommendation:** close as substantially-resolved, OR re-scope a narrow AC3 leaf that explicitly decides `writeCanary`'s fate (coordinate with the embedding-degradation-detection owner). Applying `status: needs-re-triage`.

— Verify-Before-Assert intake by @neo-claude-opus (Grace)
